desc: Guilin is one of China's signature karst destinations, pairing river cruises, cave systems, and rice-terrace excursions with easy onward access to Yangshuo. The scenery is year-round, but spring and autumn tend to balance visibility and comfort best, while late spring and early summer bring heavier rainfall and humidity.
hls: Li River boat journeys to Yangshuo
Two Rivers and Four Lakes night views

todo.name: Li River cruise to Yangshuo
todo.desc: Take the classic route past limestone pinnacles, fishing villages, and postcard river bends.

todo.name: Reed Flute Cave visit
todo.desc: Walk illuminated chambers filled with stalactites and dramatic rock formations.

todo.name: Elephant Trunk Hill viewpoint
todo.desc: See Guilin’s best-known natural symbol where rock arches resemble an elephant drinking from the river.

todo.name: Cycle Yangshuo countryside
todo.desc: Rent bikes for village roads between karst towers, bamboo groves, and river crossings.

todo.name: Longji Rice Terraces day trip
todo.desc: Head into the mountains for layered paddies and minority villages, especially striking in planting and harvest seasons.

todo.name: Evening Two Rivers and Four Lakes cruise
todo.desc: Enjoy lit bridges and waterfront landmarks after dark in central Guilin.

wifi.note: Most midrange and upscale stays have stable Wi-Fi, though cross-border app access may require preplanned connectivity tools.

prac.fltNote: From Gatwick, Guilin is usually reached via one or two connections through major hubs (e.g., Doha, Guangzhou, Shanghai, Beijing). Return fares are often ~£450–£700 off-peak, ~£700–£980 in shoulder demand, and ~£980+ around major holiday travel periods.
prac.visa: China entry requirements vary by passport and policy period; confirm current visa or transit-eligibility rules before departure.
alerts: Late spring/early summer can bring very heavy rain that affects river conditions and visibility.
National-holiday windows, especially early October, drive significant domestic demand.
Rural day-trip logistics are easiest with pre-booked transfers or guided transport.

sweet: April–May and October–November usually offer the best mix of scenery, river conditions, and manageable heat.
